commands.review_summary:
The PR reclassifies Cloudflare-like 403 HTML as `upstream_html` and changes Codex ChatGPT model calls plus WHAM usage probes to send browser-like request headers.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +11. Total +44 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live end-to-end reproduction was established. The classifier boundary is source-reproducible because current main maps status-403 HTML to `auth_html`, and the linked issue supplies the observed Cloudflare challenge text.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling remains because the contributor must supply live provider-path proof and maintainers must choose header mitigation versus classifier-only scope.

target.title: fix: browser-like Codex headers + Cloudflare challenge classification (#94432)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenAI/Codex OAuth login completes successfully, but codex/* model calls fail with an "Authentication failed" error because chatgpt.com/backend-api returns an HTML Cloudflare browser-challenge page (HTTP 403) instead of an API response. The openclaw/(os release; arch) User-Agent and missing browser-standard headers trigger Cloudflare bot detection.

Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94432

## Why This Change Was Made

Three-part fix (v2 addresses ClawSweeper review findings):

1. **Cloudflare challenge classification.** Expanded detection to align with shared STANDALONE_HTML_ERROR_HINT_RE in src/shared/assistant-error-format.ts. Now catches cdn-cgi/challenge-platform, challenge-error-text, cloudflare, captcha, attention required in addition to the original three markers. This prevents gaps where a 403 challenge page with shared markers but without the original patterns would still fall through to auth_html.

2. **Browser-like HTTP headers for Codex requests.** Chrome-like User-Agent + standard browser headers (Accept, Accept-Language). The UA override is applied AFTER provider attribution policy resolution (resolveProviderRequestHeaders) to bypass the OpenAI attribution policy openclaw/... UA injection at provider-attribution.ts:528.

3. **WHAM cooldown probe header sync.** probeWhamForCooldown in src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts now uses the same browser-like headers — same chatgpt.com/backend-api/wham/usage endpoint, same contract.

## User Impact

- Accurate error messages: Cloudflare blocks show as CDN/gateway errors, not auth failures
- WHAM quota probes share the same header contract as Codex model calls
- No behavior change for generic 403 HTML (still auth_html), non-Codex providers unaffected

## Evidence

**137 tests, 0 failures across 4 test suites (worktree):**

    provider-error-patterns.test.ts: 40 passed
    openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts: 14 passed
    provider-usage.fetch.codex.test.ts: 9 passed
    usage.test.ts: 74 passed (includes WHAM probe header assertions)

**Key assertions verified:**
- Cloudflare challenge 403 HTML (cdn-cgi/challenge-platform, Enable JavaScript...) -> upstream_html
- Generic 403 HTML -> auth_html (no regression)
- Codex usage fetch UA -> contains Mozilla/5.0 (post-attribution)
- WHAM probe UA -> contains Mozilla/5.0 (post-attribution)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Codex OAuth calls to chatgpt.com/backend-api blocked by Cloudflare browser-detection; errors misleadingly blamed authentication.

**Real setup tested**: Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112), Node.js v24.13.1, openclaw main @ 41691a82d5.

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run       src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts       src/llm/providers/openai-chatgpt-responses.test.ts       src/infra/provider-usage.fetch.codex.test.ts       src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts

**After-fix evidence**: 137 tests pass; classification + header contract assertions all green.

**Observed result**: Cloudflare challenge pages with any of the 8 aligned markers are classified as upstream_html instead of auth_html. Browser-like headers are set post-attribution-policy on all three chatgpt.com/backend-api paths.

**What was not tested**: Live Codex OAuth session — this environment cannot reach chatgpt.com (corporate network restriction). The header change follows the same pattern used successfully by web_fetch.ts (Chrome-like User-Agent for Cloudflare-protected endpoints).

## Risk checklist

- [x] Backward-compatible; generic 403 HTML still auth_html
- [x] Accept overridden by SSE/WebSocket builders — no conflict
- [x] Cloudflare markers aligned with shared detector
- [x] WHAM probe shares browser-like header contract
- [x] No new dependencies

## Changes from v1 (ClawSweeper P1 findings addressed)

| ClawSweeper Finding | Resolution |
|---------------------|-----------|
| P1: Cloudflare markers too narrow | Added 5 shared markers: cdn-cgi/challenge-platform, challenge-error-text, cloudflare, captcha, attention required |
| P1: Codex usage test assertion broken | Updated assertion to Mozilla/5.0 in both fetch.codex.test.ts and usage.test.ts |
| P1: WHAM cooldown probe stale UA | Synced to browser-like headers, same post-attribution pattern |
| P1: UA overridden by attribution policy | Moved UA to AFTER resolveProviderRequestHeaders() — discovered provider-attribution.ts:528 injects openclaw/... UA |
target.author: sunlit-deng
